tool.name: whichdb
tool.summary: Report bounded provider-discovery routes for one provider-qualified query

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `whichdb` reports deterministic provider-discovery rows for one provider-qualified accession or identifier. The EMBOSS-RS v1 surface normalizes the provider prefix and provider-local query, then reports the governed retrieval or metadata methods that can be used next. The method is a route-reporting seam: it tells the caller which shipped method family can handle a bounded provider route, rather than retrieving data itself.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts exactly one provider-qualified query such as `ena:AB000263`, `ncbi:protein:NP_000537.3`, or `sra:SRR000001`. The provider prefix is lower-cased; the provider-local query is trimmed and otherwise preserved, including nested provider-local database qualifiers after the first colon. Bare identifiers, local file paths, inline sequence literals, and unqualified database names are rejected because this seam does not perform database-universe discovery.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a stable table report with provider, normalized query, route label, discovery status, and next governed methods. Supported ENA, NCBI, and SRA provider routes return bounded routing guidance. Unsupported provider prefixes are still reported as syntactically valid rows with `unsupported_provider` status and an empty next-method list, so callers can distinguish unsupported scope from parse failures.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs whichdb`. Rust service coverage exercises the governed service route for a supported ENA query and verifies that unsupported providers are reported without fallback.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 `whichdb` seam is intentionally bounded. It does not perform live provider search, payload retrieval, archive download, local file indexing, broad database discovery, or a fallback chain across provider universes. Unsupported-provider rows document that the provider is outside the shipped bounded surface; they are not retrieval promises.

artifacts.label: Bounded ENA whichdb route fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed case note for the bounded ENA provider-discovery route exercised by Rust service coverage.

artifacts.label: Bounded NCBI whichdb route fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed case note for preserving nested NCBI provider-local database qualifiers in bounded route reporting.

artifacts.label: Unsupported-provider whichdb route fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed case note for explicit unsupported-provider reporting without fallback expansion.

examples.title: Report the bounded ENA provider-discovery route
examples.description: Normalizes an ENA-qualified query and reports the governed retrieval or metadata methods available for that provider-qualified route.

expected_outputs.label: Bounded whichdb route table
expected_outputs.description: A stable table row with provider `ena`, normalized query `AB000263`, route label `ena.sequence-or-archive-discovery`, discovery status `supported_provider`, and next methods `seqret,runinfo,runget,infoassembly`.

examples.title: Report the bounded NCBI reference-sequence route
examples.description: Normalizes an NCBI-qualified query while preserving the nested provider-local database qualifier after the first colon.

expected_outputs.label: Bounded NCBI whichdb route table
expected_outputs.description: A stable table row with provider `ncbi`, normalized query `protein:NP_000537.3`, route label `ncbi.reference-sequence-discovery`, discovery status `supported_provider`, and next method `refseqget`.

examples.title: Report unsupported providers without fallback expansion
examples.description: Reports a syntactically valid but unsupported provider-qualified query as an explicit no-fallback row.

expected_outputs.label: Unsupported-provider whichdb route table
expected_outputs.description: A stable table row with provider `uniprot`, normalized query `P12345`, route label `unsupported-provider`, discovery status `unsupported_provider`, and no next methods.
